x-enumeration-literals.name: Fixed Amount Debit Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The debit amount is a predefined fixed value set for the transaction or service.

Comment:
Example:
Monthly account maintenance fee of $10 automatically debited from a checking account.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Percentage-Based Debit Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The debit amount is calculated as a percentage of a base value, such as transaction amount, account balance, or loan amount.

Comment:
Example:
Debit 2% of a transaction amount as a service charge.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Tiered Debit Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The debit amount is determined based on tiers, slabs, or ranges of transaction amounts or balances.

Example:
$5 fee for withdrawals up to $500, $10 for $501$1,000, and $15 for above $1,000.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Periodic Debit Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The debit amount is calculated based on time, duration, or period elapsed.

Comment
Example:
Daily interest on an overdraft or monthly service fees.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Conditional Debit Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The debit amount is calculated only when certain conditions or events are met.

Comment:
Example:
Debit a penalty fee if an overdraft occurs or if minimum balance is not maintained.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Dynamic Debit Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The debit amount is determined using a mathematical formula or dynamic calculation that may include multiple variables.

Comment:
Example:
Debit calculated as: (Transaction Amount × 1.5%) + $2 processing fee.
